channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Sleep expert answers questions on adjusting to permanent daylight saving time in B.C. and improving sleep habits
item.description: In a live Q&A published by The Globe and Mail, sleep expert Dr. Rébecca Robillard, a clinical neuropsychologist and associate professor at the University of Ottawa, addressed reader concerns about sle...

item.title: Nigeria dey attract global luxury brands due to rising digital economy and wealthy youth
item.description: Naija dey turn one big target for global luxury brands as dem dey look new markets beyond Europe, North America and some parts of Asia. Indian entrepreneur, Prateek Suri, talk say the young population...

item.title: President Marcos Holds Bilateral Meeting with UN Secretary-General Guterres in New York, Discusses Security Council Bid
item.description: President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. held a bilateral meeting with United Nations Secretary-General António Guterres at the UN headquarters in New York on March 9, 2026. This took place during the President...

item.title: Delays and exaggerations in Essex supercomputer project raise questions over UK AI investments
item.description: The planned Essex supercomputer, touted in government announcements as a cutting-edge facility due by the end of 2026, remains undeveloped, currently functioning as a scaffolding and scrap metal depot...

item.title: KNEC Confirms KCSE Kenyan Sign Language Graded Only for Hearing-Impaired Candidates
item.description: The Kenya National Examinations Council (KNEC) has clarified that Kenyan Sign Language (KSL) exams in the KCSE are strictly meant for learners with hearing impairment. This comes after some confusion ...

item.title: Arusha Prepares for IPU Meeting and AFCON 2027 with Infrastructure Upgrades
item.description: Arusha region is preparing to host the Inter-Parliamentary Union (IPU) meeting in August 2026, and this event is expected to boost trade and tourism as hundreds of parliamentarians from all over di wo...

item.title: Iran outlines its response strategies towards US and Israel tensions
item.description: The news report focuses on Iran's current geopolitical stance and strategies in response to perceived aggression from the United States and Israel. According to the coverage, Iran is adopting a multif...

item.title: Review of ABC National Forum: A cautious approach to community discussion
item.description: The ABC’s new program, ABC National Forum, hosted by David Speers, premiered as the network’s intended replacement for Q+A, though ABC insists it is not a direct successor. The show adopts a 'town squ...

item.title: Beth Confesses to Lying in Edong Case in 'What Lies Beneath' Episode 101
item.description: In Episode 101 of 'What Lies Beneath,' Beth courageously admits that she lied in the case involving Edong. Her confession marks a pivotal moment in the ongoing drama, as it brings clarity to the event...

item.title: Kenya Enacts National Infrastructure Fund to Boost Major Development Projects
item.description: President William Ruto has signed the National Infrastructure Fund (NIF) Bill, 2026 into law at a breakfast event at State House Nairobi, attended by government officials and private sector leaders. T...

item.title: G7 ready to support global oil supply amid US-Israel-Iran conflict
item.description: The G7 countries don meet virtually to discuss the wahala wey dey unfold for global oil market as the war involving United States, Israel and Iran don push oil prices sharply higher. Even though dem t...

item.title: President Ruto allocates KSh 15-20 billion from Kenya Pipeline IPO proceeds for JKIA expansion
item.description: President William Ruto has announced that Kenya will use part of the proceeds from the initial public offering of the Kenya Pipeline Company to fund the expansion of Jomo Kenyatta International Airpor...

item.title: ACI FMA Commits to Strengthening Tanzania’s Financial Markets
item.description: Dar es Salaam – ACI Financial Market Association (ACI FMA) Tanzania has confirmed its dedication to boost the country’s financial markets, aiming to make them a central driver of sustainable economic ...

item.title: Medieval recognition of sign language enabled deaf people to marry and access legal rights
item.description: In February 2026, Leicester Cathedral held a British Sign Language service to mark the 450th anniversary of a deaf marriage from 1576. The groom, Thomas Tilsey, a deaf blacksmith from Leicester, excha...

item.title: Anthropic Sues U.S. Government After Pentagon Labels It a Supply Chain Risk
item.description: Anthropic, the AI company behind the chatbot Claude, has filed lawsuits against the Trump administration following its blacklisting by the Pentagon. The Department of Defense labeled Anthropic a 'supp...

item.title: Mamelodi Sundowns to face RB Leipzig in friendly match in Pretoria
item.description: Mamelodi Sundowns, perennial champions of the South African Premier League, have announced a high-profile friendly against German Bundesliga side RB Leipzig. The match is scheduled for 29 May at the L...

item.title: Upper gastrointestinal cancers surpass lung cancer as leading cause of cancer deaths in Australia
item.description: For the first time, upper gastrointestinal (GI) cancers have overtaken lung cancer to become the leading cause of cancer-related deaths in Australia, according to a recent analysis by the Pancare Foun...

item.title: SADC Finance Officials Review Current Budget and Discuss Plans for 2026/27
item.description: On March 9, 2026, senior finance officials from Southern Africa met in Pretoria for a key session of the SADC Finance Committee. The meeting was attended by representatives from 11 of the 16 member st...

item.title: Ukraine Provides Expertise to Countries on Countering Iranian Drones
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy announced that eleven countries have sought Ukraine’s assistance in defending against Iranian-designed drones, which are now being launched by Russia and used i...

item.title: Ireland to invest in vegetable farming and horticulture in Arusha, Tanzania
item.description: Tanzania dey ready to welcome more Irish investment, especially for horticulture and vegetable farming, to boost economic growth and create jobs. Ireland’s Ambassador to Tanzania, Nicola Brennan, meet...
